topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elara scrolled through her feed, a faint furrow forming between her brows. She’d been trying to ignore the persistent notification from Professor Albright’s account, a notification that seemed entirely out of place amidst her carefully curated study schedule. It was a picture of a rare orchid, captioned with a philosophical quote. Elara tapped it, her thumb hovering over the ‘unfollow’ button. Professor Albright, a man whose lectures were notoriously dry and whose office hours were a labyrinth of complex theories, was not known for this kind of public display. A strange knot tightened in Elara’s stomach. She checked the profile again. There, in the comments section, was a comment from Albright himself, praising a student’s essay. Elara leaned back in her chair, the plastic groaning under her weight. *Just a coincidence,* she told herself firmly. *Maybe he’s just trying to build a professional brand.* But the way his eyes seemed to linger on her recent project, the way his posts occasionally seemed tailored to the specific topics she was struggling with—it all felt too precise.

[story 2]

Mark felt a sudden, sharp prickle on the back of his neck as his phone buzzed. It was a direct message from Ms. Davies, his history teacher. The message was a picture of a dusty old map, accompanied by a cryptic note about forgotten civilizations. Mark stared at the screen, his jaw tightening. He was convinced it was a prank, perhaps a misplaced photo shared by someone else. He glanced towards the doorway, half expecting her to walk in, but the hallway remained empty. He minimized the chat window, his fingers tracing the edge of the screen repeatedly. Why would Ms. Davies be sharing esoteric content on a platform usually reserved for official school announcements? He felt a distinct chill despite the warmth of the room. He pulled up her main profile, a quick scan revealing an unnerving consistency in her posting habits. It was a peculiar pattern, one that made him want to question everything he thought he knew about their relationship.

[story 3]

Liam watched the screen, his breath catching in his throat. It was Professor Vance. Vance, the sternest academic in the department, had just posted a short video clip of himself hiking in the mountains. Liam blinked, rereading the caption, which was surprisingly lighthearted. He tried to dismiss it; Vance was known for his rigid adherence to academia, not outdoor adventures. Yet, the video quality was high, and the setting seemed eerily familiar—the same remote trail they had discussed in class last semester. A slow, creeping unease began to settle over Liam. He didn't move to close the application. Instead, he zoomed in on the timestamp. It was dated just hours before Liam had submitted his final paper for the course. A hollow feeling bloomed in his chest, a sense that the carefully constructed facade of their professional distance was suddenly, profoundly cracked.
